title: I am a calm, capable sleeper
subtitle: A present-tense affirmation for building the identity before the evidence is fully there.
excerpt: A short affirmation from Day 20 of the SLEEP program. For when the old story of being a bad sleeper runs ahead of the current reality.
sourceNotes: Adapted from Day 20 of SLEEP: A 30-Day Tapping Intensive (Rebecca J Page, 2025).

practice.whenToUse: Use in the morning as a statement of identity that you're building toward, not one you've already earned.

content.text: A short affirmation from Day 20 of the SLEEP program. The line claims a new identity — not a prediction about tonight, but a statement about who you are in the process of becoming.
I am a calm, capable sleeper. My choices reflect that identity today. Consistency makes it real.
Say it three times in the morning, hand on heart. The practice is in claiming it before the evidence is complete — that's how the identity builds.
Adapted from Day 20 of SLEEP: A 30-Day Tapping Intensive (Rebecca J Page, 2025).
